seo.title: Inflation Calculator | Numerly
seo.description: Estimate future cost, cumulative inflation, and purchasing power loss over time.

intro: Measure how inflation changes future prices and erodes purchasing power.
explanation: This calculator applies compound inflation over years to estimate future amount and purchasing power change.

formula.expression: Future Cost = Current Amount × (1 + inflation rate)^years

steps: Derive purchasing power and value increase
example.result: You get future cost, cumulative inflation percentage, and purchasing power estimate.

faqs.a: It is total percentage increase in prices across the full period.
No, it projects using a fixed assumed inflation rate.
It estimates how much present money can buy in future terms.
Yes, then future cost equals current amount.
It helps set realistic long-term savings and spending targets.

instructions: Derive purchasing power and value increase
formulaExplanation: This calculator applies compound inflation over years to estimate future amount and purchasing power change.
edgeCases: Very long terms can significantly increase interest cost.
Zero or near-zero rates reduce compounding impact.
Always verify units (monthly vs annual) before interpreting output.
practicalTips: Compare at least two scenarios before making decisions.
Use conservative assumptions for rates and growth.
Review total cost, not just monthly amount.
